Title: A telomere-to-telomere gap-free assembly integrating multi-omics uncovers the genetic mechanism of fruit quality and important agronomic trait associations in pomegranate

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Moshiliu; M0109; Sanbai; additional black/red/white cultivars for promoter PCR
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Determines peel and other tissue coloration and fruit commercial quality
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Lower PgANR expression is associated with black peel by limiting conversion of anthocyanins to proanthocyanidins, thereby favoring anthocyanin accumulation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PgANR expression was significantly lower in black-peel cultivar Moshiliu than in red or white cultivars. 2. A unique repeat expansion/replacement in the PgANR promoter was identified specifically in black-peel types. 3. The promoter structural variation was proposed to reduce promoter activity, lower PgANR expression, and thereby produce the black peel phenotype by limiting anthocyanin-to-proanthocyanidin conversion.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in red and white peels than in black peels
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in M0109 and Sanbai peels; lower in Moshiliu peels at 30, 50 and 120 DAF
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Anthocyanidin reductase; reductase involved in flavonoid conversion
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative structural variation analysis; promoter PCR validation; RNA-seq expression profiling; qRT-PCR; metabolite profiling of anthocyanins/proanthocyanidins
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native PgANR promoter with repeat expansion variant
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome resequencing/GWAS-related variant analysis; RNA-seq; metabolite profiling; BioProject PRJCA032027, PRJCA032035, PRJNA1184194
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promoter variant is a candidate marker for black peel selection and could be targeted to modulate anthocyanin versus proanthocyanidin balance in breeding
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May shift flux from proanthocyanidins toward anthocyanins
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although ANR is an enzyme in flavonoid metabolism, the paper primarily emphasized promoter variation and expression effect rather than direct enzymatic characterization; categorized here as Common Gene per study framing around trait-associated structural variation.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Determines tissue coloration and contributes to fruit quality and commercial value
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes conversion of leucoanthocyanidins to anthocyanidins in the late anthocyanin biosynthetic pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor of target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established as rate-limiting, but demonstrated as essential for anthocyanin production in this study
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of PgANS function or expression causes anthocyanin deficiency and white coloration in leaves, flowers, peel and arils
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GWAS and RNA-seq identified PgANS as the strongest candidate gene for white peel/anthocyanin-deficient phenotype. 2. A 37.2-kb chromosome translocation disrupted the PgANS coding sequence and was present in all tested anthocyanin-defective cultivars. 3. CRISPR/Cas9 knockout of PgANS in pomegranate caused anthocyanin deficiency, validating PgANS as essential for anthocyanin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in coloured peels than white peels
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly upregulated in coloured peels relative to white peels at 30, 50 and 120 DAF
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit developmental stage (30, 50, 120 DAF); 9% sucrose medium revealed red pigmentation in WT Nana but not knockout lines
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS; LD mapping; RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; PCR screening of translocation boundary; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; Sanger sequencing of edited alleles; phenotype and anthocyanin profiling
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native promoter/expression context in pomegranate; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout construct targeting exon 1
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: GWAS/resequencing; RNA-seq; metabolite profiling; BioProject PRJCA032027, PRJCA032035, PRJNA1184194
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful causal gene and structural marker for selecting against anthocyanin-less phenotypes; can be edited or preserved to control pomegranate coloration
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function eliminates pigmentation across multiple tissues
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study argues the causal lesion is a translocation rather than the previously proposed insertion.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: pomegranate accessions including Moshiliu, M0109, Yi-3, Sanbai, Kai Feng Si Ji Hong, M1028; tomato Micro-Tom
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: lignified secondary cell wall material determining seed hardness
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Controls inner seed coat thickening and seed hardness, thereby affecting eating quality
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NAC transcription factor with NAM DNA-binding domain
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Secondary cell wall thickening/lignification in the inner seed coat
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: PgNST3G activates the PgMYB46 promoter; the PgNST3A E56K variant loses this activation ability
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major causal regulator for seed hardness/soft-seed trait identified by GWAS, expression analysis, allelic association, and functional assays
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: The E56K mutation in PgNST3 is tightly linked to soft seed by impairing promoter binding/activation of PgMYB46 and reducing inner seed coat secondary wall thickening
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GWAS mapped seed hardness to PgNST3, where a G-to-A SNP at coding position 166 causes an E56K substitution in the conserved NAM domain. 2. This SNP predicted soft- versus hard-seeded phenotype with 100% accuracy in 80 accessions. 3. PgNST3G, but not PgNST3A, activated and bound the PgMYB46 promoter in LUC and EMSA assays. 4. Overexpression of PgNST3A in tomato produced smaller and softer seed coats, supporting causality of the soft-seed allele.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: PgNST3 expression correlated with seed hardness phenotype and was upregulated in soft-seeded inner seed coat
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In pomegranate inner seed coat, expression changed across 20, 30, 50, 60/90, 120 DAF and was higher in soft-seeded accessions; transgene expressed in tomato overexpression lines
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed developmental stage (20-120 DAF in pomegranate); tomato plants grown under 16:8 h light/dark, 25°C
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Developmental control during seed coat formation; possible autoregulation affected by E56K mutation
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Possible autoregulatory control of its own promoter was affected by the E56K mutation, as discussed with Figure S21
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS; LD mapping; RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; Sanger genotyping in natural accessions; transgenic overexpression in tomato; dual-LUC assay; EMSA
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter in tomato overexpression and dual-LUC effector constructs; native PgMYB46 promoter in reporter assay
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: GWAS/resequencing; RNA-seq; BioProject PRJCA032027, PRJCA032035, PRJNA1184194
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Excellent marker for soft-seed breeding; allele-specific selection or editing of PgNST3 offers a route to improved eating quality
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Soft-seed allele may reduce secondary wall thickening and seed structural robustness; overexpression of PgNST3A in tomato reduced seed coat size/hardness
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper discusses similarity to Arabidopsis NST3/NST1 T94K-like mechanism; E56K both elevates PgNST3 expression and abolishes transactivation of downstream targets.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: lignified secondary cell wall material determining seed hardness
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to inner seed coat thickening and hardness
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Secondary cell wall transcriptional program downstream of PgNST3
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Acts as direct downstream target activated by PgNST3G
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Validated as direct downstream target mediating PgNST3 control of seed hardness pathway
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely promotes inner seed coat wall thickening downstream of PgNST3, but was not itself functionally edited or overexpressed in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PgMYB46 was identified among five candidate downstream TFs. 2. PgNST3G directly activated the PgMYB46 promoter, whereas PgNST3A failed to do so. 3. Loss of PgNST3A binding to the PgMYB46 promoter provides mechanistic explanation for soft seed phenotype.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Predicted from expression pattern and SNBE motif analysis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter SNBE prediction; dual-LUC assay; EMSA
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native PgMYB46 promoter in LUC reporter
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential downstream engineering target for tuning seed coat hardness, though direct trait validation remains incomplete
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because the Results section experimentally validated it as the direct target in the causal PgNST3 module, though it is not the primary GWAS-hit gene.
